author: jsign
content: BTW, if you have a runnable ELF for Zisk that passes at least some of the zkevm@v0.4.1 release, I'm quite interested in it. I've setup a Hive-like runner for guest programs, so I could try it out :)

author: 0xrubo
content: i just run those zkevm tests on x64 machine just to see how our stateless execution is doing -- it passed 1383 out of 1395. i can provide you the elf to try play around on zisk if you wish.

author: jsign
content: Can you compile for zisk v0.18.0?

author: 0xrubo
content: sure, we already migrated to v0.18.0

author: 0xrubo
content: how to send it to you?

author: jsign
content: Whatever way you prefer. You can send me a link to it (here or via DM, whatever is good to you)

author: jsign
content:
BTW, would be possible that the Nethermind repo could create zkEVM releases which publish these ELF as artifacts?
Ideally we would like to make ere-guests detect new releases and pull the ELF and re-publish so we have a central repository for ELFs. (e.g. publish some `guest-program_ziskv0.18.0.elf` or similar (prob we have to think the naming a bit more)).

(Not required for this ELF, you can send me any link -- mostly for your awareness of the pipeline we would like to have)

author: 0xrubo
content: sure, we plan to include it with regular releases when we're satisfied with the quality -- passing these tests 100% would be it (it's 99%, we fail 12). or you mean a separate, zkevm-dedicated release pipeline?

author: jsign
content:
Any kind of release should be good for us if has the required artifact.

I was thinking of a separate release than regular re-execution Nethermind since prob that makes it more clear/easier to do a new release of the guest program without adding noise for regular Nethermind users? (IIUC what you meant by "regular releases") (and viceversa)

author: jsign
content: But whatever feels good to you is fine. Maybe we might only have some opinionated take on how to name stuff (mostly to standarize the naming convention, but we can decide that later cc <@466102668138512394>  for visiblity)

author: 0xrubo
content: you got me right on regular releases. given that zkevm stuff moves/changes faster at the moment and also it depends on zkvm releases (zisk for us), your suggestion of a dedicated release flow makes a lot of sense
